chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Constitutional Petition No.D-5989 of 2023 M/s. New ABC Neelam Ghar Versus The Federation of Pakistan and others DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S). Direction 1. For orders on Misc. No.8654/2024. 2. For orders as to maintainability of petition..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-. Dated 09.10.2024 None present for the petitioner. Mr. Imdad Khan, advocate for Respondents No.3 and 4. Ms. Wajiha Mahdi, D.A.G..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-. None present for the petitioner. On the last date too petitioner’s counsel was called absent. There are questions regarding maintainability of this petition as noticed on the first date of hearing that is 12.12.2023 when the notices were ordered. Respondents’ counsel claimed that the appellate remedy is not exhausted as required and this petition is not maintainable in view of the efficacious remedy available. Be that as it may, since this is a second date of hearing when petitioner’s counsel is called absent, the petition is dismissed for non-prosecution along with pending application(s). CHIEF JUSTICE JUDGE Ayaz Gul
